$comment_schema: Cross-impl golden vectors for the Pages Tag + HasProperty filter primitives (#1908 slice 2, increment c). Shape: { rows[], scenarios[] }. Each row is a page carrying a set of tags and a list of typed properties; scenarios apply an AND-composed list of FilterPrimitive wire objects and assert the SET of matching page ids. Only the matching-id set is compared cross-impl (representation-stable). Asserted by BOTH src-tauri/src/commands/tests/pages_tag_property_conformance_tests.rs (real query path) and src/lib/tauri-mock/__tests__/tag-property-conformance.test.ts (mock metaRowMatchesFilter / hasPropertyMatches).
$comment_scope: Covers Tag plus the HasProperty predicates the mock actually implements: Exists, NotExists, Eq, Ne — for BOTH Text and Ref values. The ordered/LIKE predicates (Lt/Gt/Lte/Gte/Contains/StartsWith) are DELIBERATELY excluded: the mock's hasPropertyMatches returns true (default branch) for them, a known un-implemented gap tracked by a separate #1908 follow-up. Including them here would (correctly) fail the conformance gate. Tag reads block_tags (mock: blockTags map); HasProperty reads block_properties (mock: properties map). Ref values compare value_ref, Text values compare value_text.
$comment_seeding: Both impls seed mock/db state directly (no canonical fixtures). Rust: block_tags rows reference tag blocks (block_type='tag'), block_properties Ref values reference a real target block — both inserted via INSERT OR IGNORE before the join row, because foreign_keys=ON. Mock: blockTags.set(id, Set(tags)) and properties.set(id, Map(key -> {value_text|value_ref})), cleared per scenario.

scenarios.name: Tag t-missing — no page carries it
HasProperty Ne Text status=done (absent OR different)
HasProperty Ne Ref assignee=REF01 (absent OR different)
AND-composition — Tag t-work AND HasProperty Exists status
